title: The Moles' Intergalactic Snack Run
summary: Two adventurous moles named Disco and Disco discover a hidden red button while digging beneath a garden. Pressing it launches them into space aboard a shiny metal rocket ship stocked with 'Zingos'—a delicious snack of worms and ants. Guided by Captain Izzy, the crew navigates to the planet Sauthica to deliver life-saving sandwiches to the tiny, starving Mookers. After a quick visit to the Odic Aquarium to see a giant Angler fish, the brave explorers return safely to Earth just in time for bed.

highlightQuote.text: Zingos are little worms and ants, moles!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. I'll turn on the stars, and turn off the light. Now where do you want to sit for stars and stories? Do you want to lie down?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to stand.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. I'm going to sit over here by the chair. You can come sit on my lap if you want.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want a Hatty Mousey story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a Hatty Mouse story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, I changed my mind for a mole story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a mole story. Now, once upon a time...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A mischief mole story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The mischief moles, Disco and Disco, were digging a new tunnel, deep underneath the garden. Dig, dig, dig through the dirt. And suddenly, their claws came upon something new. Tap, tap, tap. It was something red, and it was round. And Disco said, "What do you think that is?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It must be a flower.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "No, it's not a flower. It almost looks like a button. I wonder if we should..." and just as he was saying that, thinking to himself, "I wonder if we should press it," you know what Disco did?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He pressed the button. Click. And then for a second, nothing happened. But then they heard a rumble, rumble, rumble, rumble. And the whole tunnel started to shake and shiver and rumble around them. And then they were shooting up out of the ground, sitting on top of a shiny metal space rocket! And what did they say about that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yippee!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "Wow, our very own space rocket was buried under the garden all this time and we didn't even know it." And Disco says, "I wonder if we should go on a space mission." But you know what Disco was already doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He had already opened the door and stuck his nose inside the rocket. And what was inside the rocket ship?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, there was a control panel and two little mole-sized seats. And what else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some... some nut-things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, where did they store the snacks on the rocket ship?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They stored them in a cabinet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, there's a cabinet for snacks. And what snacks did the rocket ship have?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zingos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What are Zingos?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They're little, they're little, they're little worms and ants.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "Look at this! In this cabinet, there's Zingos." And Disco said, "Oh, those are my favorite..."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zingos are little worms and ants, moles!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds delicious. And you know what else they found? A space phone. And Disco was looking at the space phone when suddenly he heard a sound. It was like this: [beeping]. And he said, "What does that mean?" And Disco said, "I think the space phone is ringing. Do you want to answer it?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm already answering by telephone. I'm just in my rocket ship and I'm trying to answer it, and you're trying to answer it at the same time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness. Who's calling you, Isabella?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ah, I'm trying to call the person on the telephone. But I can't hear what I'm trying to say because Mole's calling on the telephone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "Hello, hello, who's calling on the telephone? Is it someone up in space?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Who is it? Who is it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a little voice said, "Um, hello, hello! We need help! We need help! Help! Help! Help! Help!" And Disco said, "Who's that?" And the little voice said, "It's us, the Mookers. You know, the fuzzy, funny, furry floopers." And Disco said, "The Mookers?" "Yeah, we live on the planet of the Mookers." And Disco and Disco said, "I've never heard of that." "Well, we need your help because we're very hungry. Do you have any snacks?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you like worms or ants?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What do you like?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Us... um, sandwiches.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know, but maybe you like Mooker food?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, Mooker food. Mooker food is a sandwich.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bingo! But we're far away on our own planet. Can you fly your rocket ship to us?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know what direction you are and I did bring my map. Oh yeah, use your map and try to bring us a snack please. Thank you. Bye bye.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ah, but I didn't bring one. Not a single map's in my pocket.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "That's okay. Do you have one, Disco?" Ah, well we can look around the ship and see if we can find one. Hey, there's this other cabinet. Maybe there's maps in here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I found one, I found one, I found one. Go down, go down, go down.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What does it show, Captain Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It shows that the Mooker planet... and there's Irma, and Uranus, and Mika, and Anna, and Sheila, and Mosha, and Akira, and Susy, and finally the planet of the Mookers is the Trongiand. So, and it's the right, and now I know, and now I know what direction, and now I know which planet, and now I know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so you can help us get there, Captain Izzy? You can fly our rocket ship?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. The right, the way. And the planet of the Mookers is Sauthica.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Is what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sauthica is the planet of the Mookers and that's planet really close to our house. So we only have to fly on Cornell Street and then Kitridge, and then onto Causey, then on Marigold, and then that's the street of the Mookers. And there's their planet and if you see it, we're... we can help you out with the car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, the rocket.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The rocket.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, Captain Izzy, get in the captain's chair and press the button to make the rocket ship blast off.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Beep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there's a big rumbling thunder sound and the rocket ship blasted off. And they flew up into the air, and the garden below them just became tinier and tinier. And then they saw all the forests and rivers and oceans of the planet Earth get smaller and smaller below them until they were up above the clouds and the planet Earth was just a tiny little ball beneath them. And then they went on Cornell Street, and then on Kitridge Street, Marigold Street, until they found the Mookers' street, which was also where their planet was. And what did the planet of the Mookers look like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was on Marigold Street. And I helped you out the car, Disco. I helped you out the car. Oh... Disco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What about me, said Disco?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I got you out already. You and Disco. I got you out already. So, Mookers, let me find the sandwich. Cause I got it. Here you go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the Mookers said, "Thank you!" And one sandwich was enough for lots and lots of Mookers because Mookers, as we know, are tiny fur-balls. Each of them is only the size of your big toe.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was reaching for this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did you bring over?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was reaching for this because I have to put on a different show. Now I want to fly to the Odic Aquarium.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the Odic Aquarium?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But already on the planet because it's close to the Mookers' house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Let's fly there. Land. We landed.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's go to the Odic.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what's on this planet, Captain Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I need a light, Dad. I need a light.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Don't you have a flashlight in your bed-shelf?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do. I do. I do. I do.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait. Fishies. Oh, there's a spot. I see them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You see them? What do you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I see lots of fishes, even one big Angler. Let's put that back up there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Disco said, "You know, we're a little sleepy. This has been a great space mission, but I think it might be time to fly back home. Will you take us there, Captain Izzy?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, we landed on Earth already.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank goodness. Okay, we're going to get out of the ship and go back to our house. Thanks for the space mission, Captain Izzy. Goodnight. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can I do some shadow puppet?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, if you look up at the ceiling instead of down at the light, it'll be better.

characters.name: Disco (Mole 1)
characters.description: A mischievous mole who enjoys digging and accidentally launches a space mission.

characters.name: Disco (Mole 2)
characters.description: The second mole who joins the intergalactic voyage.

characters.name: Captain Izzy
characters.description: A determined space explorer and brilliant researcher, this confident commander leads her mission into the stars with absolute authority. A master pilot of both spacecraft and deep-sea submersibles, she navigates glittering heavens and crushing depths with unerring precision. Her tireless quest to collect alien artifacts remains fueled by a singular, inquisitive goal: finding playmates across the universe.

characters.name: The Mookers
characters.description: Hailing from a neighboring planet, these toe-sized spheres of dense fur bear the uncanny features of miniature moose. They communicate through a rhythmic chorus of "mooks" and are defined by a singular, voracious obsession with sandwiches.

characters.name: Angler
characters.description: A giant fish seen at the Odic Aquarium.

places.name: Underground Tunnel
places.description: Tucked behind Goofy the Gopher’s overstuffed armchair and a bookshelf of weathered lore lies a yawning, meticulously carved excavation. This plunging vertical shaft—the very site where industrious moles labored until their claws struck the cold hull of a buried rocket—spirals downward from the cozy retreat as a gateway into a vast and echoing subterranean world.

places.name: Space Rocket
places.description: A shiny metal vessel buried under the garden.

places.name: Sauthica
places.description: The planet of the Mookers, located just a few streets away from the garden.

places.name: Cornell Street
places.description: Bisected by a stark yellow line, this celestial artery cuts through a dense forest to chart a path toward the Mookers' planet. A cosmic thoroughfare where the infinite is stitched into the earthly, it anchors local commerce—typified by a towering tree of honey—alongside the residence of Uncle X, the house of the surreal bedroom feast, and Hattie’s home. It remains the vital threshold where the mundane pulse of the woods meets the stars, marking the point where Hattie begins her journey to find medical help.

places.name: Odic Aquarium
places.description: A planetary destination filled with exotic fish.

places.name: Planet Earth
places.description: The home planet where the garden and the moles' house are located.
